output:
## Solution

**At t = 0:** 5 cables remain, each bearing **9.4 tons** with **10-ton capacity**.

**Fatigue:** Each cable's capacity decreases as 10 − 0.5t (where t is in hours).

**Failure condition:** capacity drops to the current load of 9.4 tons.

$$10 - 0.5t = 9.4$$
$$0.5t = 0.6$$
$$t = 1.2 \text{ hours}$$

All 5 cables are identical and bear the same load, so they all hit the failure threshold **simultaneously**. The moment any one fails, the load redistributes to 4 cables: 47/4 = **11.75 tons each** — far above the survivors' already-degraded 9.4-ton capacity. Instant cascade.

**The bridge collapses 72 minutes after Cable 1 snaps.**
